NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STAY WRIT OF EXECUTION PENDING APPEAL WITHOUT FILING BOND (TRANSACTION ID # 60840561) FILED BY PLAINTIFF FINEGOLD, BRADLEY A. HEARING SET FOR AUG-11-2017 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRADLEY FINEGOLD'S MOTION TO STAY WRIT OF EXECUTION PENDING APPEAL WITHOUT FILING BOND IS GRANTED. EXECUTION OF THE JUDGMENT IS STAYED PENDING THE RESOLUTION OF THE APPEAL FILED BY MR. FINEGOLD. DEFENDANTS' REMEDY FOR THEIR SOLE GROUND TO OPPOSE THIS MOTION (I.E. THAT THE APPEAL WAS UNTIMELY FILED AND IS INVALID) IS A MOTION TO DISMISS THE APPEAL, WHICH IS PENDING IN THE COURT OF APPEAL. IF THE MOTION TO DISMISS IS GRANTED, THE STAY WILL CEASE TO HAVE ANY FORCE AND EFFECT. IF THE MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED, THE STAY WILL REMAIN IN EFFECT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (302)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Writ Of Execution Pending Appeal Without Filing Bond
Off Calendar Aug-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Albert M. Kun of the law offices of Albert Miklos Kun (415-362-4000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF BRADLEY FINEGOLD's Motion To Stay Writ Of Execution Pending Appeal Without Filing Bond is GRANTED. Execution of the judgment is stayed pending the resolution of the appeal filed by Mr. Finegold. Defendants' remedy for their sole ground to oppose this motion (i.e. that the appeal was untimely filed and is invalid) is a motion to dismiss the appeal, which is pending in the Court of Appeal. If the motion to dismiss is granted, the stay will cease to have any force and effect. If the motion to dismiss is denied, the stay will remain in effect. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Apply 180 Day Period To Appeal For Failure To Apply Proper Notice Of Entry Of Amend Or In The Alternative Pursuant To Ccp 1294
Off Calendar Jun-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Albert Kun, Esq. (415-362-4000) for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court sympathizes with Counsel but the California Supreme Court has determined that the 60-day appeal period is jurisdictional. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRADLEY FINEGOLD'S Motion For Order To Apply 180 Day Period To Appeal For Failure To Apply Proper Notice Of Entry Of Amend Or In The Alternative Pursuant To Ccp 1294 is DENIED. None of Mr. Finegold's arguments have merit. The declaration of Mr. Kun, Mr. Finegold's counsel, does not explicitly explain what page was assertedly missing from the notice of entry of judgment. Defendant Merrill Lynch presents competent evidence that a complete copy of the notice of entry of judgment was mail and e-served and the notice of entry of judgment e-served with the court is complete. Mr. Kun's unfamiliarity with the e-filing process is no basis for relief because the 60-day appeal period is jurisdictional and mistake is no excuse. (Conservatorship of Townshend (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 691, 700-701; In re Hanley's Estate (1943) 23 Cal.2d 120, 123. CCP 1294.4 applies only to appeals involving claims under the Elder and Dependent Protection Act and nothing in the statute allows the trial court to extend the appeal period. CCP 36(a) allows a party to obtain a trial preference and nothing in that statute allows the trial court to extend the appeal period.
(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Albert Kun, of Law Offices, Tel. 415.362.4000, for plaintiffs Bradley Finegold;
Spencer Persson, of Norton Rose Fulbright US LLP, Tel. 213.892.9200, for defendants Merrill Lynch and Thomas White.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. THIRD PARTY THOMAS WHITE, MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH, INC. Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award is granted. As explained in the companion motion to vacate, there is no basis to vacate the arbitration award. Order and judgment signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, Email: geales@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Vacate Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Albert Kun, of Law Offices, Tel. 415.362.4000, for plaintiffs Bradley Finegold;
Spencer Persson, of Norton Rose Fulbright US LLP, Tel. 213.892.9200, for defendants Merrill Lynch and Thomas White.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BRADLEY FINEGOLD Petition To Vacate Contractual Arbitration Award is denied. CCP 998(b) expressly provides that it applies to arbitration proceedings. Mr. Finegold cites Pilimai v. Farmers Ins. Exchange (2006) 39 Cal.4th 135. That decision holds that CCP 998 applies to uninsured motorist arbitrations. Mr. Finegold cites no authority holding that CCP 998 does not apply to FINRA arbitrations. Mr. Finegold fails to demonstrate that his rights were prejudiced by the arbitrators' failure to continue the hearing. The hearing was originally scheduled for April 2016. The arbitrators continued the hearing from April 2016 to September 14, 2016. On August 22, 2016, Mr. Finegold sought to continue the hearing to serve subpoenas on entities that might have information regarding Mr. Finegold's bonds. The arbitrators properly denied the continuance request. The matter had already been continued, there was no explanation regarding why Mr. Finegold waited so long to seek the discovery, and the discovery request was after the 20-day witness/exhibit list deadline. The arbitrators did not abuse their discretion in denying the continuance request and Mr. Finegold also fails to demonstrate substantial prejudice from the denial. (See SWAB Financial v. E*Trade Securities (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 1181, 1198.) The arbitrators did not exceed their authority in hearing the arbitration even though Mr. Kun filed for bankruptcy. Mr. Finegold executed the underlying arbitration agreement. The submission agreement that Mr. Kun executed to start the arbitration was on behalf of Mr. Finegold. In other words, Mr. Kun was acting as Mr. Finegold's agent. Mr. Kun's personal bankruptcy is irrelevant as the arbitration award is against Mr. Finegold. Arbitrator Dietrich's disclosure or lack of disclosure is not a basis to vacate the award. There is no evidence that the 2010 lawsuit involved Mr. White. Mr. Kun makes the assertion on information and belief. (Kun Dec., par. 17.) Arbitrator Dietrich also made an updated disclosure that affirmed that he had no relationship with Mr. White. (Persson Dec., ex. 17.) The court rejects Mr. Finegold's argument that the arbitrators failed to comply with the ADA. First, it is unclear which of the enumerated grounds to vacate the award per CCP 1286.2 Mr. Fineman is relying upon. Second, Mr. Kun admits that he received an earphone. Third, he does not explain what he failed to hear or how the result of the arbitration would have been different. The court rejects Mr. Fineman's argument that the CCP 998 offer was grossly inadequate. Mr. Fineman does not cite an enumerated ground to vacate the award under CCP 1286.2. Moreover, this appears to be a mistake of law argument and that is not a basis to vacate an award. (See Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 11.) Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, Email: geales@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
